Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed declarative clause; noun class 12 subject 'akamwa' + genitive construction + object marker -mu- + verb root -rya (present habitual).
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A person’s own words can cause them harm.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, moral advice, politics, family disputes.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in Kiga oral advisory traditions where elders warned youth about reckless speech in clan meetings.
First Example Sentence: Yamugambire nabi omu lukiiko, baitu akamwa k’omuntu kamurya.
Second Example Sentence: Okwesiga ebigambo eby’enshonga zitari zo nikyo, akamwa k’omuntu kamurya.
Third Example Sentence: Abantu abavuga munonga batamanya ngu akamwa k’omuntu kamurya.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative declarative clause; noun class 12 subject + negative marker te- + verb -tera + object 'nkuba'.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: One person alone cannot accomplish big tasks.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Omuntu omwe tasobora kukora ekintu kinene wenka.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to communal farming practices in Kigezi where cooperation ensured harvest success.
First Example Sentence: Tukwatanise omu murimo, akaboko kamwe tekatera nkuba.
Second Example Sentence: Omu kyaro kyaitu tumanya ngu akaboko kamwe tekatera nkuba.
Third Example Sentence: Abashomesa barabwira abana bati akaboko kamwe tekatera nkuba.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative clause; abstract noun class 14 subject + negative marker ti- + verb -ica + object 'nfu'.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Poverty does not kill; resilience matters more.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Obworo tiburugaamu kufa butazira amaani g’omutima.
Usage Contexts: Encouragement during hardship, sermons, motivational talks.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common in post-colonial Kigezi narratives emphasizing endurance during economic struggle.
First Example Sentence: N’obu tutari n’ebingi, obworo tibwica nfu.
Second Example Sentence: Nyoko ahora abwira abana ngu obworo tibwica nfu.
Third Example Sentence: Yasingwire ebizibu byona n’akumanya ngu obworo tibwica nfu.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative declarative clause; noun class 3 subject + negative marker ti- + verb -rora + object 'kisa'.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A wicked heart cannot recognize kindness.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Omuntu omubi tasobora kubona obulungi omu bantu.
Usage Contexts: Conflict resolution, religious discourse, moral commentary.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Embedded in church teachings and clan-based moral instruction.
First Example Sentence: Yamukoreire ebirungi byingi baitu omutima omubi tigurora kisa.
Second Example Sentence: Abantu bamwe tibashimira, omutima omubi tigurora kisa.
Third Example Sentence: N’obu wamufasha, omutima omubi tigurora kisa.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative clause; noun class 12 subject + negative marker ti- + verb -renga + locative object.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A child’s authority does not surpass elders.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Omuto tasobora kurenga abakuru omu by’okucwamu.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in clan governance structures valuing elder authority.
First Example Sentence: Abazaire bamurabura bati akati k’omuto tikarenga orugo.
Second Example Sentence: Omutabani yateera empaka, bamwibutsa ngu akati k’omuto tikarenga orugo.
Third Example Sentence: Mu maka gaitu, akati k’omuto tikarenga orugo.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Relative emphasis structure; noun class 9 subject + emphatic particle 'niyo' + verb -twara + abstract object.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Wealth facilitates social relationships and hospitality.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Historically tied to bride price traditions involving cattle in Kigezi.
First Example Sentence: Omukwe yaletere ente nyingi, twamanya ngu ente niyo etwara obugyenyi.
Second Example Sentence: Abantu bamwesiga ahabw’obugaiga bwe, ente niyo etwara obugyenyi.
Third Example Sentence: Mu birori eby’obufumbo, ente niyo etwara obugyenyi.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Declarative clause; noun class 12 subject + verb -tambuka + instrumental phrase.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: People act according to their nature or upbringing.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from agrarian bird imagery common in Kiga folklore.
First Example Sentence: Omutabani arikukora nk’ishe, akanyoni katambuka n’ekishwi.
Third Example Sentence: Yarereirwe omu maka ag’omutima mwiza, akanyoni katambuka n’ekishwi.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Relative clause with demonstrative pronoun + copulative structure.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ebintu ebitaheirweho biragaruka kandi bisaba kushembererwa.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in post-conflict reconciliation discourse in southwestern Uganda.
First Example Sentence: Obuteekateeka butari bulungi, ebyo ebitaramire nibyo biramwa.
Second Example Sentence: Yareka ensonga etarashobororwa, ebyo ebitaramire nibyo biramwa.
Third Example Sentence: Mu byobufuzi, ebyo ebitaramire nibyo biramwa.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative clause; noun class 1 subject + negative marker ta- + verb -kurira + locative phrase.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects communal child-rearing norms in Kigezi villages.
First Example Sentence: Abazaire bagume nibamanya ngu omwana takurira omu nju emwe.
Second Example Sentence: Mu kyaro kyaitu, omwana takurira omu nju emwe.
Third Example Sentence: Abashomesa bafasha abazaire ahabw’okumanya ngu omwana takurira omu nju emwe.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Contrastive temporal clause; noun class 1 subject + negative marker ta- + copulative omission.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Today’s situation may not remain the same tomorrow.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Popular in contemporary socio-political commentary in Kigezi.
First Example Sentence: N’obu arikushobora munonga hati, owa leero taba owa nyencakye.
Second Example Sentence: Tumutinya, owa leero taba owa nyencakye.
Third Example Sentence: Abakuru bavuga bati owa leero taba owa nyencakye.
